chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – The silence and preservation of the tongue

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور معتب ر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/223)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور معتب ر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/223)
text: سَمِعْتُ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ كَانَ الرَّجُلُ مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ إِذَا أَرَادَ الْعِبَادَةَ صَمَتَ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ عَشْرَ سِنِينَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad and Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, altogether from Al Washha who said,
‘I heard Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘It was so that the man from the Children of Israel, whenever he intended the worship would stay silent before that, for ten years’.
en.sarwar: 18. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad and al-Husayn ibn Muhammad from Mu’alla ibn Muhammad all from al-Washsha’ who has said the following: “I heard al-Rida (a.s.) say, ‘In the tribe of Israel if a man wanted to become a worshipper he was to keep silent for ten years before starting the deed.’”
